title: Unmanifest stage to manifest stage by KRSNA's Will | SB 3.26.9 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 26 and Text 9
Devam Utero Acha, Pragyute Purushashi Abhi, Dakshinam Purushottama,
Dvokini Karan Yodasya, Saddha Satyam Yadatma Kam,
Devam Utero Acha, Pragyute Purushashi Abhi, Dakshinam Purushottama,
Dvokini Karan Yodasya, Saddha Satyam Yadatma Kam,
Purushottama, O Supreme Personality of Godhead,
Asya of this creation, Sat Asat,
Translation, purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishya Prabhupada,
Kindly explain the characteristics of the Supreme Person,
For both of these are the causes of this manifest and unmanifest creation,

material energy is impregnated and then everything is born of the material nature.
It is also confirmed in the 9th chapter of Bhagavad Gita that under His glance, Adhyakshena,
under His direction and by His will, nature is working.
It is not that nature works blindly.
After understanding the position of the conditioned souls in relation to material nature, Devahuti
wanted to know how nature works under the direction of the Lord and what the relationship
is between the material nature and the Lord.
In other words, she wanted to learn the characteristics of the Supreme Lord in relation to the material
The relationship of the living entities with matter and that of the Supreme Lord is not

by spiritual nature nor by material nature. He is the supreme controller himself and he cannot

Two words used in this verse are Sat and Asat. The cosmic manifestation is Asat, it does not exist
but the material energy of the supreme lord is Sat or ever existing. Material nature is ever
existing in its subtle form as the energy of the lord but it sometimes manifests this non-existent
temporally existent nature, the cosmos. An analogy may be made with the father and mother.
The mother and father exist but sometimes the mother begets children.
Similarly, this cosmic manifestation which comes from the unmanifest material nature of
the supreme lord sometimes appears and again disappears but the material nature is ever exist.
And the lord is the supreme cause for both the subtle and gross manifestations
So after hearing the relation between the living entities and the Prakruti, Devahuti asked this question, he is seeking the knowledge of relationship between Krishna and the material nature.
Prakruti Purusha Syaapi Lakshanam Purushottama. So nature and living entities, their relationship is understood. And now nature and the Supreme Lord, Prakruti Purusha.
And then he is asking those Lakshanam characteristics of that relationship. And he is asking directly to Purushottama.
Lord Kapila, Krishna. And these two, that means the Supreme Lord and his energies are responsible, causes for the manifest and unmanifest creation.
So we are living in this material world. So it is important that we understand this world.
Directly from the Supreme Lord. So that we can relate with it. Carefully relate with this intelligently.
So here it is mentioned, this material world has two types of manifestation. Or rather, there are two types of creation.
There is a manifest world and unmanifest material world.
So whatever we are seeing is manifest to our eyes, our senses.
But in this material world there are certain portions.
And they are not visible to us. That is unmanifest world.
This unmanifest portion is known as Mahatatva.
And from the Mahatatva comes the material manifestation.
So in this creation, the first creation is Unmanifest Creation.
And from the unmanifest creation, things get manifested.
And this Mahatatva is present in each one of our bodies.
Mahatatva is present in each one of our bodies.
Mahatatva is present in each one of our bodies.

From Pramahatvatva comes the whole manifestation of this material world and Mahatvatva also enters into each and every body. In our bodies also it is there. That's called Chittam. That's called contaminated consciousness.
So, this is Mahatvatva. And as you see in the creation, first is unmanifest creation and then comes manifest creation.
So, say for example you see somebody behaving in a certain way. What you are seeing is manifest creation.
But behind this manifest creation there is an unmanifest creation. What he thinks in his mind, what is he creating in the Chittam, that is called unmanifest creation.
Which we will not be able to see. But for anything to appear manifest, there has to be unmanifest work also.
So, if we want to change our japa quality, if we want to change our service quality, first you have to work on your unmanifest creation.
If you can create in the unmanifest.
What you want to do, how you want to serve, then automatically it becomes manifest.

The only way is by this transcendental sound.

And how unmanifest can be changed? By transcendental sound.

So today the divine disappearance Devshri Madhvacharya there are some pastimes of Shri
Padmadhvacharya from Saitanya Charitamruta let us remember the the glories of the Acharya
the Parampara Shri Padmadhvacharya took his birth near Udupi which is situated in the
South Kannada district of South India just west of Sahyadri this is the chief city of
the South Kannada province and is near the city of Mangalore which is situated to the
South of Udupi near the city of Udupi is a place called Pajaka Kshetra where Madhvacharya
took his birth near Udupi which is situated to the South of Udupi near the city of Udupi
He took his birth in a Shivali Brahmana dynasty as the son of Madhya Geha Bhatta in the year
According to some he was born in the year 1160 Shakaabdha.
In his childhood Madhvacharya was known as Vasudev and there are some wonderful stories
It is said that once his father had piled up many debts, Madhvacharya converted tamarind
seeds into actual coins to pay them off.
When he was five years old he was offered the sacred debt.
A demon named Maniman lived near his abode in the form of a snake and at the age of five
Madhvacharya killed that snake with the toe of his left foot.
When his mother was very much ill, Madhvacharya was taken to the hospital.
When Madhvacharya was very much disturbed, he would appear before her in one jump.
He was a great scholar even in childhood and although his father did not agree, he accepted
sannyas at the age of twelve.
Upon receiving sannyas from Achutaprekasha, he received the name Purnaprajna Teertha.
After travelling all over India, he finally discussed scriptures with Vidya Shankara,
the exalted leader of Sankhya Rimata.
Vidya Shankara was a great scholar.
Vidya Shankara was actually diminished in the presence of Madhvacharya.
Accompanied by Satyatidha, Madhvacharya went to Badrikashrama.
It was there that he met Vyasadeva and explained his commentary on the Bhagavad Gita before
Thus he became a great scholar by studying before Vyasadeva.
By the time he came to the Anandamata from Badrikashrama, Madhvacharya has finished his
His companion Satyatidha wrote down the entire commentary.
When Madhvacharya returned from Badrikashrama, he went to Ganjama, which is on the bank of
river Godavari, where he met with two learned scholars named Shobhanabhatta and Swami Shastri.
Later these scholars became known in the disciplic succession of Madhvacharya, as well as to
When he returned to Udupi, he would sometimes bathe in the ocean.
On such occasion, he composed a prayer in five chapters.
Once while sitting beside the sea, engrossed in meditation upon Lord Sri Krishna,
he saw that a large boat containing goods for Dwaraka was in danger.
He gave some signs by which the boat could approach the shore and it was saved.
The owners of the boat wanted to give him a present
and at the time Madhvacharya agreed to take some Gopi Chandan.
He received a big lump of Gopi Chandan and as it was being brought to him,
it broke apart and revealed a large deity of Lord Krishna.
The deity had a stick in one hand and a lump of food in the other.
As soon as Madhvacharya received the deity of Krishna in this way,
The deity was so heavy that not even 30 people could lift it.
Yet Madhvacharya personally brought this deity to Udupi.
Eight of Madhvacharya's Sanya's disciples became directors of his eight monasteries.
Worship of the Lord Krishna deity is still going on at Udupi
according to the plans Madhvacharya established.
The acharyas of the Madhva Sampradaya established Udupi as the chief center
and the monastery there was known as Madhva Sampradaya.
Madhva Sampradaya was known as Uttaradhi Matha.
Vaishnaveramadhye Rama Upasaka Sabha Keha Tattvavadi Kehahaya Sri Vaishnava
At the time, all the South Indian Vaishnavas were worshipers of Lord Ramachandra.
Some were Tattvavadis and some were followers of Ramanujacharya.
Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur points out that the word Tattvavadi refers to the followers of Shilamadhvacharya.
To distinguish his disciplic succession from the Mayavadi followers of Shankaracharya,
Shilamadhvacharya named his party the Tattvavadis.
Impersonalist monists are always attacked by these Tattvavadis who attempt to defeat their philosophy of impersonalism.
Generally, they establish the supremacy of Tattvavadis.
Actually, the disciplic succession of Madhvacharya is known as the Brahma-Vaishnava sect.
That is the sect coming down from Lord Brahma.
Consequently, the Tattvavadis or followers of Madhvacharya do not accept the incident of Lord Brahma's illusion,
which is recorded in the tenth canta of Srimad Bhagavatam.
Shilamadhvacharya has purposefully avoided commenting on that portion of Srimad Bhagavatam.
In which Brahma Mohana, the illusion of Lord Brahma, is mentioned.
Shilamadhavendra Puri was one of the Acharyas in the Tattvavada disciplic succession.
And he established the ultimate goal of transcendentalism to be attainment of pure devotional service, love of Godhead.
Those Vaishnavas belonging to the Gaudiya Sampradaya, the disciplic succession following Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu,
are distinct from the Tattvavadis although they belong to the same Tattvavada Sampradaya.
The followers of Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu are therefore known as the Madhva-Gaudiya Sampradaya.
Main purpose of Shri Madhavendra Puri accepting Madhvacharya Sampradaya.
Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu wanted to point out to the Tattvavadi Acharya who belong to the Madhvacharya Sampradaya,
that the general behavior of the Tattvavadis did not favor pure devotional service,
which must be devoid of the taints of fructive activity and speculative knowledge.
The Tattvavada Sampradaya of the Madhvacharya school sticks to the principle of Varnashtram Dharma which involves fructive activity.
Their ultimate goal Mukti is simply a form of material desire.
A pure devotee should be free from all kinds of material desire.
He simply engages in the service of the Lord.
Nonetheless, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was pleased that the Madhvacharya Sampradaya or the Tattvavada Sampradaya accepted the transcendental form of the Lord.
This is the great qualification of the Vaishnava Sampradayas.
Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu points out that Shri Madhavendra Puri accepted Madhvacharya
only because the Sampradaya accepted the transcendental form of the Lord.
There is one glorification by Srila Balade Vidyabhushan from Pramaya Ratnavali.
Ananda, Anandatiddhanama, Sokamaya, Dhama, Yathirjiyat, Samsaararnava, Tarani, Yam Iha, Jana, Kittayanti, Budha.
All glories to Shri Srila Madhvacharya Swami.
Those who are learned and intelligent glorify him.
For he is like a boat which enables the conditioned souls to cross beyond this realm of repeated birth and death.
This verse offers glorification of Shri Madhvacharya known as Anandatidha.
He is described as the dwelling place of bliss and victory.
The wise recognize him as a divine boat that helps souls cross the world.
He is the first ocean of repeated birth and death.
Shri Srila Madhvacharya Thirobha Mahamahasa Khija.
